name: Arcade (Modern Python 2D Game Library)

performance.notes: OpenGL via pyglet backend; batch sprite rendering; 60fps for typical 2D games

agent_readiness.notes: Modern, batteries-included Python 2D game library. class MyGame(arcade.Window): def setup(self): self.sprite_list = arcade.SpriteList(). def on_update(self, delta): self.sprite_list.update(). def on_draw(self): arcade.start_render(); self.sprite_list.draw(). arcade.Sprite('player.png', scale=1.0). Tilemaps: arcade.load_tilemap('map.tmx'). Physics: arcade.PhysicsEnginePlatformer. Particle effects. Sound. GUI with arcade.gui. Type hints throughout. Better documented than pygame. Built on pyglet. Modern Python patterns.

gotchas: Requires display — headless rendering not built-in
Tiled map format (.tmx) dependency for level editing
best_when: Indie 2D games wanting modern Python patterns over pygame
Platformers and top-down games with tilemap support
When pygame's legacy API is frustrating
avoid_when: Headless game simulation for RL (use pymunk or custom rendering)
summary: Arcade is a modern Python 2D game library with clean APIs and excellent docs. class MyGame(arcade.Window): setup(), on_update(dt), on_draw(). arcade.Sprite(), SpriteList(). Tilemaps via Tiled (.tmx). PhysicsEnginePlatformer. Built on pyglet (OpenGL). Type hints throughout. Better documented and more modern than pygame. MIT license. Best for teaching Python game dev and indie 2D games. Smaller community than pygame but growing.
